brand.name: Decoder
brand.tagline: Transform source code into human-readable knowledge

landing.hero: More and more code is written by <1>AI</1>. You still need to <2>understand it</2>.
landing.heroSubtitle: Decoder is a personal study project, free and open source (MIT). Maintained in spare time by a single author with help from the community. Not a product, not a commercial service: just public code to read, fork and understand. BYOK or local-inference mode.
landing.providersStrip: Works with the providers you prefer
landing.feature1Body: English, Italiano, 简体中文 and more. Switch language in one click.
landing.feature2Body: OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter — or local with Ollama / LM Studio.
landing.feature3Body: Local mode: AI inference runs in your own environment via Ollama / LM Studio; file bodies are not sent to AI providers. Uploaded files still live in your private server storage.
landing.howIntro: Three steps from source to knowledge.
landing.step1Body: Upload a ZIP or import a public GitHub repository. Files stay in your account.
landing.step2Body: OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter — or local with Ollama / LM Studio.
landing.step3Body: Human or technical summaries, quality and security analyses, Markdown export.
landing.integrationsBody: Works with the AI providers you already use and with your public GitHub repositories.
landing.whyNowTitle: Generating code is easy. Understanding it isn't.
landing.whyNowIntro: AI assistants ship code faster than any human team can review line by line. Decoder closes that gap.
landing.whyNow1Title: A growing share of code is AI-generated
landing.whyNow1Body: Copilot, Cursor, Lovable, Claude Code — an increasing portion of every new project wasn't written by the people who have to maintain it.
landing.whyNow2Body: More AI output means more diffs to read. Without a comprehension layer, reviews go shallow or get skipped.
landing.whyNow3Body: Decoder is an open tool, in users' hands, to question AI answers instead of trusting them blindly. The analysis rules grow with the community.

osStrip.title: A guardrail in your hands, not in the AI's
osStrip.body: Decoder is public, inspectable, forkable code. As AI sources evolve, the guardrail evolves too — because we build it together.

guardrail.title: An open-source guardrail for the generative-AI era
guardrail.intro: The more AI writes code, the more we need an independent tool to read it, question it and understand it. Decoder is that tool — open, inspectable, continuously evolving.
guardrail.point1Body: Every AI answer is a hypothesis, not a verdict. Decoder gives you the tools to question it line by line.
guardrail.point2Body: Open code, open prompts, open rules. No black boxes: read it, change it, fork it.
guardrail.point3Body: As models change, the analysis rules are extended by the community. The guardrail grows with the people who use it.

community.title: Built by the people who use it
community.body: Decoder gets better when users contribute. Open an issue, propose a new analysis rule, translate the UI: every contribution strengthens the guardrail for everyone.
community.cta1Body: Report a bug, propose an idea, discuss a product choice.
community.cta2Body: Analysis prompts are in the clear. Extend them to cover new risks or new languages.
community.cta3Body: Bring Decoder to your language by adding a file in src/i18n/locales.

mockup.summaryBody: This function authenticates a user by verifying email and password. Returns the user if credentials are valid, otherwise throws an error.
mockup.architectureBody: Part of the authentication module, interacts with the users database.

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.intro: Decoder is a personal, free and open-source project, maintained on a non-professional basis by a single author with voluntary contributions from the community. It is not a commercial service, involves no payment and creates no supply contract between the author and the user. Use of the software is governed solely by the MIT License and by these informational notes.
terms.natureBody: Decoder is not a product, not a commercial SaaS, and not an online platform within the meaning of Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 (DSA). The author develops it as a personal study experiment, with no business organization and no profit motive. Any publicly hosted instance is a demonstration demo provided "as is", with no availability or continuity guarantee, and may be suspended or taken down at any time without notice. The author does not act as a "trader" under EU consumer law: the conformity guarantees of Directive (EU) 2019/770 and Italian Consumer Code (Legislative Decree 206/2005, artt. 135-octies ff.) do not apply.
terms.licenseBody: Decoder is a free and open-source project released under the MIT License. The full text is in the LICENSE file shipped with the software. The project is maintained collectively by contributors ("Decoder contributors"). The entire relationship between user and author is governed by the MIT License, which expressly excludes warranties and liability to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law.

allowed.usePersonal: Use it for personal or commercial purposes
allowed.modify: Modify the source code and build custom versions
allowed.distribute: Distribute the original or modified software to others
allowed.develop: Develop new features and contribute them back

forbidden.claimCreator: Claim to be the creator of Decoder or its original author
forbidden.attribution: When forking or redistributing, fail to clearly state that it is a modified version, not affiliated with the original Decoder project

terms.ownershipBody: Copyright on original contributions remains with the respective authors and contributors. The MIT License grants you the permissions listed above without transferring copyright. There is no registered trademark holder: "Decoder" is the informal name of the open-source project.
terms.analyzedCodeBody: Any source code uploaded or analyzed via Decoder remains the property of its respective owners. Decoder does not acquire any rights over your code or the generated documentation.
terms.contributionsBody: Contributions submitted to the Decoder project (pull requests, issues, code patches, documentation) may be reviewed, merged and included in future releases at the discretion of the maintainers. By submitting a contribution you agree to license it under the same terms as the project (MIT License).
terms.dataCollectedIntro: The data listed below is processed strictly to the extent necessary to operate the demo:

dataCollected.account: Account: email, display name and creation timestamp (managed by the auth module).
dataCollected.content: Content: uploaded files kept in the server's private storage (owner-only access enforced via RLS) with an indicative 60-day retention from last activity, metadata (path, size, sha256, language, repository source — zip or GitHub) and AI explanations.
dataCollected.credentials: User API keys: encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM and never returned to the browser. BYOK is optional — Lovable AI is offered as a courtesy without configuration.
dataCollected.usage: Usage metadata: AI provider and model used for each explanation, preferred language and proficiency level, application role (e.g. admin). No behavioural profiling, no third-party tracking.
dataCollected.notracking: No advertising trackers, no third-party analytics, no profiling. The only cookie set is technical (sidebar UI preference) and requires no consent under the Italian DPA decision of 10 June 2021.

terms.managedAiBody: The default "Lovable AI" provider is offered as a courtesy because the API key is managed by the Decoder server. It is subject to usage quotas and may be disabled at any time without notice or continuity. When you use it, your code and prompts are forwarded by the Decoder server to the external gateway ai.gateway.lovable.dev, subject to Lovable's policies. You may switch at any time to your own key (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter) or to a local endpoint (Ollama, LM Studio). The author is not liable for third-party providers' processing policies.
terms.gdprIntro: Processing of personal data needed to run the demo is set up consistently with the GDPR, taking into account the project's non-professional nature.

gdpr.lawful: Lawful basis: pre-contractual measures taken at the data subject's request and legitimate interest in the technical operation of the demo (art. 6.1.b and 6.1.f); explicit consent (art. 6.1.a) when you choose an external AI provider.
gdpr.rights: Data subject rights: access, rectification, erasure, portability, objection and restriction. Export or delete your data from Settings → Account, or by opening a GitHub request.
gdpr.transfers: Non-EU transfers: only when you choose an AI provider hosted outside the EU (e.g. OpenAI, Anthropic, Lovable AI). The provider is always shown before the request runs.
gdpr.retention: Retention: data is kept until you delete your account or individual items, or until the demo is decommissioned.
gdpr.contact: The data controller is the author of the project as a private individual acting in a non-professional capacity, reachable solely through GitHub Issues or, for sensitive data, GitHub private Security Advisories. The conditions for mandatory DPO appointment (art. 37 GDPR) are not met and the exemption from the records of processing for entities with fewer than 250 employees that do not carry out risky/systematic processing applies (art. 30.5 GDPR). The right to lodge a complaint with the Italian DPA (Garante per la protezione dei dati personali) remains unaffected.

terms.aiActIntro: Decoder is an open-source application that calls third-party AI models. As a project released under a free license (MIT) and not placed on the market as a GPAI model, it benefits from the exclusion set out in art. 2(12) of the Regulation. Formal obligations for AI system providers (technical documentation art. 11, quality management system art. 17, CE marking) do not apply. The transparency obligation under art. 50 still applies.

aiAct.risk: Positioning: limited-risk downstream application. No automated decisions about people, no worker scoring, no user profiling; not among the high-risk systems in Annex III.
aiAct.transparency: Transparency (art. 50): every generated output is labelled as AI-generated content and must always be verified by a competent person.
aiAct.prohibited: Prohibited uses (art. 5): no biometric inference, social scoring, subliminal manipulation or exploitation of vulnerabilities.
aiAct.humanOversight: Human oversight: you stay in full control of when to call the AI, which provider to use and whether to accept the output.

terms.disclaimerBody: As free software offered without consideration, the general principles of Italian law on gratuitous contracts apply (notably the attenuated duty of diligence drawn by analogy from art. 1710 of the Italian Civil Code), in addition to the full warranty exclusion of the MIT License. THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHOR OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM, OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE SOFTWARE.
terms.liabilityIntro: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, and given the free and non-professional nature of the project, by using Decoder you expressly acknowledge and accept the following:

liability.aiOutput: AI output: explanations, analyses, summaries and suggestions generated by artificial intelligence may contain errors, omissions or inaccuracies. You are solely responsible for verifying, interpreting and acting on the output. The author is not liable for any decision, action or consequence based on AI-generated content.
liability.ipCode: Analyzed code and intellectual property: you warrant that you hold the necessary rights to any code or material you upload, or have proper authorization. The author is not liable for any infringement of copyright, open-source licenses, patents, trade secrets, NDAs or other third-party rights arising from the material you provide.
liability.dataTransfer: Code transmission: in cloud mode the uploaded source code is forwarded to the AI provider you select (including Lovable AI, the default managed provider that uses a server-side key held by Decoder). In local mode the uploaded files still reside in the server's storage, while AI inference runs on your localhost. The author is not liable for third-party AI providers' processing policies.
liability.security: Security and damages: the software is provided with no warranty of availability, integrity or security. The author is not liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, consequential or punitive damages — including data loss, lost profits, business interruption, bugs, vulnerabilities or malfunctions — arising from your use of or inability to use Decoder, except for wilful misconduct or gross negligence to the extent these cannot be excluded by law.
liability.unlawful: Unlawful or non-compliant use: you are solely responsible for ensuring that your use of Decoder complies with all applicable laws, regulations, contracts, corporate policies and third-party rights. The author disclaims all liability for unauthorized, unlawful or non-compliant use.

manifesto.title: An experiment for reading code together with AI
manifesto.intro: Decoder is a personal study project, open source and free. These notes describe how it is designed, why it exists and what it does not do.
manifesto.whyGuardrailBody: Generative AI can produce convincing code that still hides bugs, questionable choices or vulnerabilities. This project is a personal exercise to study whether a second reading layer — assisted by another model and using public prompts — can help understanding it. It does not replace professional tools for static analysis, security review or human code review.

guardrail.title: A study tool
guardrail.body: Decoder is a study tool, not a certified verification system. It helps reading code but does not replace your judgement or a professional review.

open.title: Open source (MIT)
open.body: MIT License: free to use, modify and distribute. No hidden components.

privacy.title: Technical measures in place
privacy.body: User API keys are AES-256-GCM encrypted and are not returned to the browser. Uploaded files are isolated per user via Row Level Security. No ad trackers, no behavioural profiling.

byok.title: BYOK
byok.body: You can configure your own key for the AI provider you prefer and pay it directly. Markdown export of explanations is available.

localFirst.title: Optional local inference
localFirst.body: When Ollama or LM Studio is selected, AI inference runs on your localhost and the file body is not sent to external providers. Uploaded files remain in the server's storage, accessible only to you.

multilang.title: Multilingual
multilang.body: Languages available today are English, Italian and Simplified Chinese. Further languages depend on community contributions.

accessible.title: Depth levels
accessible.body: You can pick the depth of the explanation, from concise to technical.

wont.w1: Uploaded code is not used to train models: the author has neither the access nor the infrastructure to do so.
wont.w2: No data sale, no marketing integrations: there is no commercial activity behind the project.
wont.w3: No AI-provider lock-in is in place; the project is BYOK.
wont.w4: Markdown export of explanations exists but remains subject to demo availability.
wont.w5: Uploaded files may be deleted automatically after inactivity; long-term retention is not guaranteed.
wont.w6: AI output must always be verified by a competent person: it is not professional advice nor a correctness guarantee.

manifesto.contributingBody: Contributions and reports are welcome via GitHub Issues and Pull Requests. The author reviews them in spare time, with no response-time commitments.

contributing.rules: Extend the analysis rules (src/lib/analysis-prompt.ts) to cover new smells or languages.
contributing.translations: Add a language by creating a folder in src/i18n/locales/.
contributing.providers: Integrate new AI providers or local engines compatible with the OpenAI API.

manifesto.roadmapBody: Areas under exploration are documented in the README and in .lovable/plan.md in the repository, with no deadlines and no delivery guarantees.

openSource.title: Public code, prompts and rules
openSource.intro: Decoder is an open study experiment: code, analysis prompts and rules live in the public repository, because that is the only way to share a personal project of this kind.

open.codeBody: The whole application, frontend and backend, under MIT.
open.promptsBody: The instructions we give to LLMs to explain, audit quality and security — editable by the community.
open.rulesBody: Finding severity, analysis kinds, aggregation criteria: all in the clear in the repository.
open.i18nBody: Three official languages today, expandable with a PR for each new language.
open.designBody: Semantic tokens and accessible components in src/styles.css and src/components/ui.

openSource.roadmapIntro: Next areas of work. Discussed in the open, editable by the community.

roadmap.r1: Extend analysis rules to new languages (Rust, Go, Kotlin, Swift).
roadmap.r2: VS Code plugin that invokes the guardrail during PR review.
roadmap.r3: Shared community library of rules for typical AI-generated code smells.
roadmap.r4: Diff-aware mode: verify only the parts changed in a PR.
roadmap.r5: Provenance: track AI origin (Copilot, Cursor, ChatGPT) block by block.

openSource.extendBody: Analysis prompts live in src/lib/analysis-prompt.ts. Add a new kind, write the instructions, open a PR: the community reviews it, we refine it together.
openSource.communityBody: Decoder is maintained by anonymous contributors. Your issue, your PR or your translation is part of the guardrail.

auth.title: Sign in to Decoder
auth.subtitle: Email/password, Google, or continue locally.
auth.signInSubline: Sign in to keep auditing AI-generated code.
auth.signUpSubline: Get an AI-aware companion for the code you didn't write.
auth.githubNote: GitHub OAuth login isn't available in this build yet.
auth.switchToSignIn: Already have an account? Sign in
auth.checkEmail: Check your email to confirm your account.
auth.disclaimerAcceptPrefix: I have read and accept the
auth.disclaimerAcceptSuffix: — including the limitations on AI output, analyzed code, security and non-compliant use.
auth.disclaimerRequired: You must accept the liability waiver to continue.
auth.disclaimerRequiredHint: Tick this box to enable sign-in and sign-up (email or Google).

dashboard.title: Your projects
dashboard.empty: You don't have any projects yet.
dashboard.deleteProjectConfirm: Delete project "{{name}}" and all its repositories, files and explanations? This cannot be undone.

howToUse.title: How to use Decoder
howToUse.intro: Pick how the AI runs. You can switch any time, per repository.
howToUse.defaultBody: Ready to use — no key, no setup. Free quota included.
howToUse.byokBody: Use OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini or OpenRouter with your own API key. You pay the provider directly, no markup.
howToUse.localBody: Run inference on your own machine via Ollama / LM Studio. AI providers do not receive file bodies. Uploaded files still live in your private server storage.
howToUse.installBody: Decoder is a web app — there's nothing to download. On Chrome/Edge desktop, click the install icon in the address bar. On iOS Safari, tap Share → Add to Home Screen.

project.noRepos: No repositories yet. Upload a ZIP or import from GitHub to get started.
project.githubOnlyPublic: Only public repositories are supported in this build.
project.retentionNotice: Files stay on the server for up to 60 days after their last explanation, then they're deleted automatically. Always export anything you need.
project.capabilityExplainBody: Human or technical summaries, tuned to your proficiency.
project.capabilityQualityBody: Code smells, dead code, bug hints and security review.
project.capabilityAiOriginBody: Estimate the probability that the codebase — or parts of it — was generated by AI.
project.howToHint: Open a repository, pick a file, and read the AI-generated comments under the Summary / Quality / Security / AI origin tabs.
project.deleteRepoConfirm: Delete repository "{{name}}" and all its files and explanations? This cannot be undone.

workspace.selectFile: Select a file to view its source
workspace.localPledge: Local mode: AI inference runs on your localhost. Uploaded files still live in the server's storage; source code is not sent to external AI providers.
workspace.noProvider: Add an API key in Settings to enable AI explanations.

howTo.title: How to start an analysis
howTo.step1: Pick a file from the tree on the left
howTo.step2: Pick an AI provider on the right

status.needFile: Select a file on the left

findings.title: Highlighted spots
findings.empty: No line references detected. Run an analysis to highlight spots in the code.
findings.jumpHint: Click a finding to highlight it in the editor

folder.title: Selected folder
folder.needsCloud: Folder analysis requires a cloud provider.
folder.noFindings: No structured findings for this file.

fix.empty: No diff yet. Run the analysis first, then generate a patch.
fix.noFindings: No findings to base the patch on.
fix.needsAnalysis: Run an analysis on this file first.
fix.needsCloud: Patch generation requires a cloud provider.
fix.howToTitle: Apply the patch to your repo
fix.how1: Save the .patch file at your project root.

analysis.needsCloud: Code analysis requires a cloud provider in this build.
analysis.empty: Run an analysis to see findings here.

aiOrigin.title: AI-origin probability
aiOrigin.intro: Estimate how likely it is that this codebase — or parts of it — was generated by an AI coding assistant.
aiOrigin.disclaimer: Heuristic estimate, not proof. Clean human code can score high; messy AI code can score low.
aiOrigin.runningHint: Analyzing source files in batches. This may take a minute.
aiOrigin.needsCloud: A cloud AI provider is required to scan the whole repo.
aiOrigin.needsCloudLong: A cloud provider (free Lovable AI or your own key) is required to analyze the codebase.
aiOrigin.unsampledNote: {{count}} additional code files were not scanned to control cost.
aiOrigin.fileEmpty: Run the AI-origin analysis on this file to see a score.

settings.title: Settings
settings.pageIntro: Profile, API keys (BYOK), local endpoints and account — all on this page. Use the links below to jump to a section.
settings.byokIntro: Decoder never stores your keys in plain text. Your code is never used for training.
settings.lovableIntro: Lovable AI is pre-configured server-side. You can start using Decoder without entering any key. Add your own keys below only if you want a different provider.
settings.lovableBadge: Ready to test — no key required
settings.localIntro: In local mode, the browser calls your localhost AI directly for inference — file bodies are not sent to AI providers. Uploaded files still live in your private server storage. You remain responsible for your local setup.
settings.accountIntro: Export everything we hold about you as JSON, or permanently delete your account at any time.
settings.deleteConfirm: Are you sure? Your account, projects, files, explanations and keys will be erased. This cannot be undone.

adminBootstrap.title: Become the first administrator
adminBootstrap.body: No administrators exist yet for this instance. Claim the admin role to manage users, roles and configuration.
adminBootstrap.done: You are now admin. Opening the panel…
adminBootstrap.notEligible: An administrator already exists. Ask one to promote you.

admin.title: Administration
admin.intro: Promote or demote administrators. Bootstrap the first admin via the SQL helper or via the "Become the first administrator" banner in Settings.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.intro: Decoder turns source code into human-readable knowledge. Read this page in your language using the switcher in the header.
docs.gettingStartedBody: Sign in, create a project, upload a ZIP or import any public GitHub repository, pick a file in the tree, then click Explain. Choose your proficiency level and human or technical summary.
docs.byokBody: In Settings → AI providers, paste a key for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini or OpenRouter. Keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM server-side and decrypted only to forward your request — they are never returned to the browser.
docs.localBody: Install Ollama (https://ollama.com) or LM Studio, then in Settings → Local AI endpoints add the base URL (Ollama: http://localhost:11434, LM Studio: http://localhost:1234) and a default model. The browser calls your machine directly for inference — file bodies are not sent to AI providers. Uploaded files still live in your private server storage.
docs.securityBody: You own your code, generated documentation and suggested comments. We never train on user repositories. Every database table uses Row-Level Security scoped to your account.
docs.adminBody: If you are the first user, go to Settings and click "Become admin" in the banner at the top. Or from the Lovable Cloud SQL editor: SELECT public.promote_to_admin('your-email@example.com');

errors.zipTooLarge: The ZIP file is too large.
errors.noFiles: No readable files found in the ZIP.
errors.notFoundBody: The address you opened doesn't exist or has been moved.

contact.title: How to reach us
contact.intro: Decoder is a non-profit open source project. All official channels go through GitHub: public for bugs, private (Security Advisories) for vulnerabilities and personal data.
contact.privacyBody: To exercise your GDPR rights (access, rectification, erasure, portability, objection, restriction) open a request on the repository. If your request contains personal data, use the private Security Advisories channel.
contact.privacyCta: Open a GDPR request on GitHub
contact.securityBody: Found a vulnerability? Report it privately via GitHub Security Advisories — the channel stays private until a coordinated fix is shipped.
contact.bugsBody: For bugs, ideas and improvements open a public issue on the repository.
contact.cookieNote: Decoder uses no analytics or third-party trackers. The only cookie set is a technical UI preference (sidebar state): no consent is required under the ePrivacy directive.

s1.title: Welcome to Decoder
s1.body: Decoder helps you understand codebases, files and code snippets by generating explanations, comments and technical insights adapted to your level.

s2.title: Choose how AI runs
s2.subtitle: Decoder supports two modes. You stay in control of where your code is processed.

local.title: Local mode
local.b1: Uses Ollama, LM Studio or another local model.
local.b2: Designed so that code is processed locally.
local.b3: Recommended for sensitive or private code.

cloud.title: Cloud BYOK mode
cloud.b2: Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini and OpenRouter.
cloud.b3: Selected code or prompts may be sent to the chosen provider.
cloud.b4: You are responsible for provider costs and provider terms.

s3.title: Add a provider
s3.subtitle: You can configure providers now or later from Settings.
s3.warning: Never paste API keys in public chats, screenshots, GitHub issues or frontend code.
s3.configureLater: You can skip this step and configure providers later from Settings → API keys.

s4.title: Import code
s4.subtitle: Choose how to bring code into Decoder.
s4.safePath: Start with the demo repository or with non-sensitive public code.

s5.title: Select analysis type
s5.subtitle: Pick the type of insight Decoder should generate.
s5.disclaimer: Decoder does not provide certified security audits or professional legal/compliance review.

s6.title: Choose reader level
s6.subtitle: Pick the audience for the explanation.
s6.body: The same code can be explained differently depending on who needs to understand it.

s7.title: Review AI output
s7.subtitle: AI is a helpful assistant — not an authority.

points.inaccurate: AI explanations may be inaccurate or incomplete.
points.verify: Verify outputs before relying on them for technical, security or production decisions.
points.actions: You can accept, reject, edit or export suggested comments.

s8.title: Acknowledgement
s8.subtitle: Please confirm you understand the following before continuing.
s8.intro: All checkboxes are required. Nothing is pre-checked.

checks.cost: I understand that I am responsible for costs generated by my API key.
checks.providers: I understand that, when using a cloud AI provider, selected code and prompts may be sent to the selected provider.
checks.authorized: I confirm that I have the right and authorization to analyze the code I upload or synchronize.
checks.review: I understand that AI outputs may be inaccurate and must be reviewed by a qualified person.
checks.terms: I accept the Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy.

actions.a2: Connect a local model if using private code.
actions.a3: Add a cloud provider only if you understand the provider's terms.
actions.a4: Run a small file analysis before analyzing a full repository.

settings.intro: Review the introduction tour and acknowledgement at any time.

byokAck.title: Important BYOK and AI Provider Notice

body.p1: You are using your own API key with a third-party AI provider.
body.p2: You are solely responsible for any costs generated with your provider account.
body.p3: Decoder does not control third-party provider pricing, billing, rate limits, data retention or model behavior.
body.p4: If you choose a cloud AI provider, selected code snippets, repository content or prompts may be sent to that provider for processing.
body.p5: Do not submit confidential, proprietary, regulated, personal, employer-owned or third-party code unless you are authorized to do so and have reviewed the provider's terms.
body.p6: In local mode, Decoder is designed so that code is processed locally, but you remain responsible for your local setup, models, plugins and network configuration.
body.p7: AI-generated explanations may be inaccurate and must be reviewed by a qualified person before any technical, security, legal, compliance or production use.
body.p8: Decoder is an open-source educational code-understanding tool and does not provide certified security audits, legal advice, compliance advice or production guarantees.

checks.cost: I understand that I am responsible for costs generated by my own API key.
checks.providers: I understand that cloud AI providers may receive the code or prompts I choose to analyze.
checks.authorized: I confirm that I am authorized to analyze the code I submit.
checks.review: I understand that AI-generated explanations may be inaccurate and require human review.
checks.terms: I accept the Terms and Privacy Policy.

settings.removeKeyWarning: Removing an API key stops all future calls to that provider until you add a new key.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Decoder is a personal open-source case study. This page summarizes what data is processed, why, and how to exercise your rights.
privacy.controllerBody: The project is operated by the individual maintainer as a private individual, not a company. Contact: open a GitHub issue or a private Security Advisory on the repository.
privacy.dataIntro: Decoder processes only what is needed to deliver the demo:

data.account: Account: email, display name, language preference.
data.files: Uploaded ZIPs / imported repository files (private bucket, RLS-scoped).
data.explanations: AI-generated explanations and suggested comments you produce.
data.keys: Encrypted BYOK API keys (AES-256-GCM) and a short key hint; provider configuration.
data.endpoints: Local AI endpoint configuration (URL and default model).
data.acks: Acknowledgement records (language, IP address, user agent, timestamp).
data.logs: Operational logs from the hosting platform may include IP address, user agent and request metadata.

privacy.purposesBody: Provide the demo features (storage, BYOK forwarding, AI explanations), prevent abuse, and keep an audit trail of consent.
privacy.lawfulBody: Performance of the service you request (Art. 6(1)(b)) and legitimate interest in operating a safe public demo (Art. 6(1)(f)).
privacy.retentionBody: Account, files, explanations, credentials and endpoints are retained until you delete them or the demo is decommissioned. Acknowledgement records and operational logs may be retained for up to 12 months as a consent / abuse-prevention audit trail.
privacy.rightsBody: Access, rectification, erasure (full account or per item: project, repository, explanation, API key), portability (JSON export), restriction, objection. Exercise from Settings → Account, or via GitHub if Settings is unreachable.
privacy.thirdPartiesBody: Hosting and database: Lovable Cloud (Supabase). AI gateway: ai.gateway.lovable.dev. Cloud AI providers you choose (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, OpenRouter) receive your selected code and prompts under their own terms. Local providers (Ollama / LM Studio) run on your machine — no AI provider transfer for inference, but uploaded files still live in server storage.
privacy.minorsBody: This demo is not directed at persons under 16. Do not create an account if you are below that age.
privacy.securityBody: BYOK keys are encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM. The credentials table has no SELECT policy. Every user-data table uses Row-Level Security scoped to auth.uid(). The service-role key is never bundled into client code.
privacy.noWarrantyBody: Decoder is provided as-is, with no warranty. It is not a certified security audit, legal/compliance advice, or production decision system.

dataFlow.title: How your data flows
dataFlow.intro: Three modes are supported. Each one routes your code differently. Local mode keeps AI inference on your machine; cloud BYOK sends selected code to the provider you chose.
dataFlow.cloudTitle: Cloud BYOK mode (OpenAI / Anthropic / Gemini / OpenRouter)
dataFlow.githubBody: You authorize GitHub. Decoder imports the metadata and file contents of the repositories you select, under the scopes you granted. You can delete imported project data at any time.

warnings.secrets: Do not upload secrets, tokens or `.env` files.
warnings.env: Remove `.env`, credentials and customer data from any archive.
warnings.terms: Review the AI provider's terms before sending real code.
warnings.demo: Start with a public demo repo or non-sensitive code.

intendedUse.title: Intended use
intendedUse.notIntro: Decoder is NOT designed or suitable for:

notItems.regulated: Automated employment, credit, insurance, health, law-enforcement or public-authority decisions.

footer.ownership: Your code and generated documentation belong to you. Decoder never trains on your repositories.
footer.disclaimer: Decoder is an open-source educational code-understanding case study. It is not a certified security audit tool, legal/compliance tool, or production decision system. AI-generated outputs may be inaccurate and must be reviewed by a qualified person.
